import java.lang.System.Logger;
import java.lang.System.Logger.Level;

// The JDK's own logging facade (JEP 264) is tier-1 like slf4j: log() on a
// receiver whose declared type resolves to java.lang.System.Logger, carrying
// the caught, at ERROR or WARNING severity.
class SystemLoggerCapture {
    private static final Logger log = System.getLogger("x");

    void errorReports() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.log(Level.ERROR, "failed", e);
        }
    }

    void warningReports() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.log(Level.WARNING, "failed: " + e, e);
        }
    }

    void reportCoversTheExit() {
        try {
            risky();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.log(Level.ERROR, "fatal", e);
            System.exit(1);
        }
    }

    void risky() throws Exception {}
}
